headline: PG&E (PCG) Stock Still Looks Cheap On Earnings While Broader Checks Look Mixed
summary: PG&E stock has quietly put up a 74.9% return over the past five years. With recent coverage highlighting it as an undervalued value pick, the key tension for investors is whether the current price already reflects most of that recovery or still leaves room based on its valuation checks. Over five years, PG&E has returned 74.9%, which puts recent share moves into context for anyone trying to judge how much of the recovery story is already priced in. Expectations for ongoing earnings growth...

headline: Is PG&E (PCG) Still Cheap Following Its Methane Milestone?
summary: Why PG&E’s methane milestone is drawing fresh attention to the stock PG&E (PCG) has attracted fresh interest after reporting a 60% cut in methane emissions from its natural gas pipeline system compared with 2015, surpassing California’s 2025 target and its own 2030 goal. For investors, this environmental milestone, combined with expectations around upcoming Q2 earnings, has put PG&E’s stock and its longer term risk profile back under the microscope. See our latest analysis for PG&E. Against...

headline: Will PG&E’s (PCG) Rapid Methane Cuts Reshape Its Risk Profile and Energy Transition Narrative?
summary: Pacific Gas and Electric Company recently reported that it has cut methane emissions from its natural gas pipeline system by 60% from 2015 levels, surpassing both California’s 2025 target and its own 2030 goal as part of a broader push toward a net zero greenhouse gas emissions energy system by 2040. This faster-than-planned emissions reduction, enabled by advanced leak detection and targeted repairs, may influence how investors assess PG&E’s operational risk profile and longer-term energy...
